item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok first, I want to say I like this :) it is an interesting idea in my opinion.

However i'd like to point out that "small square screen about 1 meter long" does not sound quite right, "a 1x1 metre (or meter) screen" fits notation better in my opinion, even if you mention it is a square, also, Im not sure that counts as a "small" screen, but that is a nit-pick

Regardless, it seems you have already improved this over time, sorry if you are done with this and this comment is pointless. But I do enjoy this scp and just wanted to point out a small detail I think would make it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept, but I have some issues with it that lead to a downvote for now.

First, I think it is too powerful an effect to be believable. The skip can erase any data, with the risk of doing so forever. Without any reliable way of limiting or reversing this effect, it becomes too dangerous for the story being told. As it is now, I think the Foundation would be much more invested in destroying it as soon as possible, or the situation would be much more dire.

Second, there are still some places where the clinical tone is let down to put emphasis on how advanced is the tech involved. This could be easily solved by changing references from 'advanced design' to 'anomalous design'.

Last and least, it should never have been Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why are you posting one-liners on dozens of articles?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is an interesting concept for somewhat poorly. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took the liberty of making two small grammar edits for sentences that appeared to simply have a few words omitted. I think they were benign and neutral enough not to cause any tonal changes or anything - just two instances of "was" and one "in which". Hope that's all right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has a few grammar slips here and there, but overall is good enough to warrant an upvote. Just reread this carefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It might be better to be clearer with the anomaly. We would want to know the weird parts of it as soon as we can, so I think the discovery can come later.

The data consumption process does seem fascinating, although the initial description of that part feels rather mundane. Later paragraphs definitely made it clear that it is anomalous, but I think the wording can be better synthesised to be clear as to why it is an SCP.

Also, it might help to see more on how does the SCP ascribe the tastes of various bits of information. That part feels to have great potential, which is sadly only mentioned and not elaborated in meaningful depth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
I want more! SCP the heck outta his friend!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made some changes based on Forty4 and Quetzhal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed that part up a bit. Hopefully it seems better now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I took your advice and made a lot of changes to it. Thank you! It has really improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When civilian internet service providers couldn't fix the issue, Foundation Agents in the area investigated and discovered SCP-3014 attempting to construct [REDACTED] but was lacking the needed tools and parts.

This needs to be expounded on more. I don't think that "bad wifi —> Foundation investigation" is believable.

I also don't like the way the robot talks.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A couple grammatical errors here and there that could be easily fixed. Other than that, I really like this SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mm. I kinda like it, but there are a few problems that lead to my current downvote. Address them and… Well, it'll be a decent enough skip to be a mainlister, I think.

By and large, I would consider this skip to be more a Thaumiel than a Euclid, and certainly more interesting if used in such a manner. I'll go further in depth in my critique below.

+ Critique
- Critique

So to start with, I'm going to say that the only thing about this skip I find interesting is the antimemetic effect it exerts on information. Containment efforts can also be interesting provided you find a better method to contain it. I can't imagine the Foundation simply throwing information at this thing - especially 500MB. It may not sound like much data, but whole 500MB is a short video, it encompasses a lot of text.

So what are a few ways to contain it? We could try feeding it other information-based entities that we need to contain. Maybe you've got a metastasized Keter-level meme that's growing too rapidly to be contained with traditional antimemetic countermeasures. Maybe you write an AI to generate stories to feed the skip - even fiction is information, albeit that sort of information might be less filling. I like seeing creative containment procedures, and I strongly suggest considering more alternatives over simply giving the skip what it wants.

There are also a couple of tone issues I would address.

The entity also possesses an antenna of a design beyond current understanding, which according to SCP-3014 is broken, and previously could broadcast information faster than light.

This doesn't really tell us anything about the antenna. What does it look like? Does it actually look damaged? Is the technology anomalous, or replicable?

SCP-3014 was recovered after several hundred residents of ████████, UK reported weak internet connects.

I think you mean "connections".

When civilian internet service providers couldn't fix the issue, Foundation Agents in the area investigated and discovered SCP-3014 attempting to construct [REDACTED] but was lacking the needed tools and parts.

Wait, why couldn't they fix the issue, and why did the Foundation step in? I'm sure the ISP will send engineers out to inspect the lines, in which case they would discover the skip first. I also can't imagine the Foundation stepping in so quickly, over what's apparently a minor problem.

I'd also remove the redaction here entirely, as it's rather pointless and doesn't contribute to any sense of mystery. It's probably building a communication array of some sort.

If SCP-3014 doesn't consume its required information, it may:

Have an increase in aggressive behavior
Show hostility towards Foundation personnel
[REDACTED]
Leak radioactive material (source unknown)
Attempt to maim or kill Foundation personnel
Attempt to leak Foundation information online
[REDACTED]

I don't really like this entire thing. Standard thing what kills you. Far more interesting if it begins to, say, create a reverse antimemetic effect that somehow caused information it has stored to begin overwriting existing information. Think of something more interesting.

That's all the advice I have. Hope it helps.

item.title: My First SCP!
item.content:encoded:
Wowza… I finally felt brave enough to put some content up on this site.

I want to thank jishtrans in the chat and someone else in the chat who left and I forgot their name. Start with M, I think.

I also want to thank the SCP community for being so open, and to all the SCPs and Tales I read over the years that have inspired me to make something!

I'm pretty nervous of putting something up, and I'm sure there is a lot of flaws in this that I missed, but I tried my hardest for my first SCP.
